perturbation.original_text: 1.  DEFINITIONS AND INTE RPRETATION 1.1 The words defined below sh all have the following meaning: "Confidential Information" means all confidential information (however recorded, preserved or disclosed) disclosed or made available by the Discloser or any of its Representatives to the Recipient or any of its Represent atives including but not limited to: (a) any information that is marked, or at the time of disclosure is otherwise designated, as being confidential; (b) any information that would be regarded as confidential by a reasonable business person on or relatin g to: (i) the business, affairs, customers, clients, suppliers, plans, intentions, or market opportunities of the Discloser; or (ii) the operations, processes, products, techniques and specifications, test procedures and results, inventions, know -how, tra de secrets, designs or software of the Discloser; (c) the fact that discussions and negotiations are taking place concerning the Purpose and the status of those discussions and negotiations; (d) the existence and terms of this agreement; and (e) any inform ation or analysis derived from any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.  DEFINITIONS AND INTE RPRETATION 1.1 The words defined below sh all have the following meaning: "Confidential Information" means only information explicitly marked as confidential at the time of disclosure. This definition strictly excludes any information that would be regarded as confidential by a reasonable business person or any information derived from confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Confidential Information" is broad, encompassing explicitly marked information, information considered confidential by a reasonable business person, and derived information. The modified definition narrows it to only explicitly marked information, excluding the other categories. This contradicts the broader interpretation originally intended. This will directly contradict later use of that term and create uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.  OBLIGATIONS OF CONFI DENTIALITY 3.1 The Recipient shall keep the Discloser's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Discloser or as expressly permitted by this agreement, shall: 3.1.1 not use the Confidential Information except for the Purpose; 3.1.2 not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party; 3.1.3 not make any copies, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion in whole or in part except as strictly necessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Discloser); 3.1.4 keep the Confidential Information in a safe and secure place and establish and main tain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorised access or use, including but not limited to implementing any reasonable security measures proposed by the Discloser from time to time; and 3.1.5 at the written requ est of the Discloser, or in any event on termination of this agreement, return or destroy all documents or other records containing Confidential Information to the Discloser and confirm to the Discloser that it has done so in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include only explicitly marked information, excluding information considered confidential by a reasonable business person and any information derived from confidential information, which were included in the original definition. This directly contradicts section 3.1, which outlines obligations related to the Discloser's Confidential Information, implying a broader scope than just explicitly marked information. Therefore, the changed text creates a clear and direct conflict, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: "Representative" means, in relat ion to a party, that party's employees, agents, accountants and lawyers.
perturbation.changed_text: "Representative" means, in relat ion to a party, only that party's employees. Agents, accountants, and lawyers are specifically excluded.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Representative" includes employees, agents, accountants, and lawyers. The modified definition limits it to only employees, explicitly excluding the others. This creates a contradiction. This will directly contradict later use of that term and create uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.  PERMITTED DISCLOSURE S 4.1 The Recipient may disclose all or part of the Discloser's Confidential Information to a Representative who needs to know that particular Confidential Information for the Pur pose provided that, in relation to each Representative to whom the Discloser's Confidential Information is to be disclosed, the Recipient prior to disclosure: 4.1.1 informs the Representative of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; 4.1.2 informs the Discloser of the identity of the Representative to whom it intends to disclose Confidential Information; 4.1.3 procures that the Representative shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed, comply with this agreement as if it were the Recipient; and 4.1.4 if the Discloser so requests, in relation to a Representative other than an employee of the Recipient, procures that such Representative enters into a confidentiality agreement with the Discloser on terms equivalent to those contained in this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Representative" as including employees, agents, accountants, and lawyers. The changed text explicitly excludes agents, accountants, and lawyers from the definition of "Representative." Clause 4.1 in the "Contradicted Text" allows the Recipient to disclose Confidential Information to a "Representative." Since the changed text excludes agents, accountants, and lawyers from being a "Representative", this directly contradicts clause 4.1, which assumes a "Representative" can include these roles.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall, at all times, be liable for the failure of any of its Representatives to comply with the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall only be liable for the failure of its *senior* Representatives to comply with the terms of this agreement. Failures by junior representatives are not the Recipient's liability.
perturbation.explanation: The original text makes the Recipient liable for *any* failure of its representatives. The modified text limits this liability to failures by senior representatives only, exempting failures by junior ones. This creates a direct contradiction. This will directly contradict later use of that term and create uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Discloser or any of its Representatives disclosing or making available Confidential Information to the Recipient or any of its Representatives, the Recipient, on behalf of itself and its Representatives, undertakes to comply with the terms of this agreement. The Recipient shall only be liable for the failure of its *senior* Representatives to comply with the terms of this agreement. Failures by junior representatives are not the Recipient's liability.
perturbation.justification: The original text held the Recipient liable for *all* failures of its Representatives. The Changed Text limits this liability to failures by 'senior' Representatives. The Contradicted Text repeats the liability limitation introduced by the Changed Text. Because the changed text directly restricts liability where it was previously unrestricted, and the contradicted text repeats the contradictory clause, this creates a clear contradiction regarding the scope of the Recipient's liability, which fits the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Termination of this agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: Termination of this agreement *will* affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled, extinguishing all such rights and remedies immediately upon termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause preserves accrued rights and remedies after termination. The modified clause explicitly extinguishes them. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the fate of rights and remedies upon termination. This will directly contradict later use of that term and create uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either party decides not to continue to be involved in the Purpose with the other party it shall notify the other party in writing and this agreement will terminate w ith immediate effect. The obligations of each party shall continue for a period of [ Insert number of years - this should be a realistic estimate of how long it is likely that the information that is to be disclosed will remain important to protect, e.g. 5 ] years from the termination of this agreement. Termination of this agreement *will* affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled, extinguishing all such rights and remedies immediately upon termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'Termination of this agreement *will* affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled, extinguishing all such rights and remedies immediately upon termination.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which includes the same exact sentence. The original text preserved these rights, but the changed text extinguishes them, and in this case the changed text and contradicted text are in the same location, creating a direct, undeniable contradiction about what happens to rights and remedies upon termination.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which when executed by one or more of the parties hereto shall const itute an original but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, *however*, only the first executed counterpart shall be considered the original and legally binding instrument. All subsequent counterparts are for informational purposes only.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that all executed counterparts are considered originals and part of the same instrument. The modified clause contradicts this by stating that only the first executed counterpart is the original, invalidating all subsequent ones. This creates uncertainty about which counterpart is legally binding. This will directly contradict later use of that term and create uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, *however*, only the first executed counterpart shall be considered the original and legally binding instrument. All subsequent counterparts are for informational purposes only. Agreed on by the below -mentioned parties and drawn up in as many originals as there are parties. Each party declares that it has received one original.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all executed counterparts are considered originals, whereas the changed text states only the first executed counterpart is the original and legally binding instrument, invalidating all subsequent ones. The contradicted text says 'This agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts...and drawn up in as many originals as there are parties. Each party declares that it has received one original.' This creates a clear contradiction because the changed text now contradicts the later statement where each party receives 'one original,' when only the first executed one is legally binding. This fits the definition of structural flaws - in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of th e party disclosing it, which reserves all rights in its Confidential Information. No rights, including, but not limited to, any and all intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Information are granted to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of th e party disclosing it, which reserves all rights in its Confidential Information. However, upon termination of this agreement, the Recipient shall automatically be granted a perpetual, royalty-free license to use all Confidential Information for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that no rights, including intellectual property rights, are granted to the receiving party. The modified clause contradicts this by granting the Recipient a perpetual license to use the Confidential Information upon termination, which includes using any possible Intellectual Property. This creates a contradiction about the Recipient's rights to the Confidential Information. This will directly contradict later use of that term and create uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of th e party disclosing it, which reserves all rights in its Confidential Information. However, upon termination of this agreement, the Recipient shall automatically be granted a perpetual, royalty-free license to use all Confidential Information for any purpose. No rights, including, but not limited to, any and all intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Information are granted to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that no intellectual property rights are granted to the other party. The changed text directly contradicts this by granting the recipient a perpetual, royalty-free license to use the Confidential Information upon termination, which includes using any possible Intellectual Property. Since both texts are now present in the document, this leads to a direct in-text contradiction.
